interpretation: 当前网站 Citrus 基线已切到 balanced_hybrid_v1。
BTC 使用更快 TP / trailing，ETH 使用 anti-chase + short_relax，SOL 保持保守 low-DD sleeve。
在统一 6000U 基准资金下，组合 PF 为 2.04，胜率为 61.46%。
US500 4H Synthetic Flow PPO true-suite 已接入网站口径。
Full backtest 为 291 笔交易，PF 0.87，Sharpe -0.64。
Walk-forward OOS 共 23 folds，累计 OOS PnL -57.70 USD，正 Sharpe fold 占比 30.4%。
Monte Carlo 1000 次重采样下，中位总收益 -76.46 USD，5% 分位 -216.45 USD。

baseline.display_name: BTC+ETH High-Chase + ETH Bucket + SOL 0.52/0.50 (No HYPE)

bridge_results.title: SOL Late-Trend Veto on Statistically Insufficient Evidence (n=7)
bridge_results.why_flagged: Extreme sample deficiency (n=7) makes variance estimates unreliable. SOL is explicitly flagged for feature redesign in current strategy memory, not additive veto logic. Validation profile 'btc_range_sync_core' incorrectly applied to SOL, which requires asset-specific robustness standards given its research-stage status.
bridge_results.hypothesis: Reduce late-trend chase losses by vetoing stretched entries near local highs.
bridge_results.risk_note: Recent Citrus memory confirms SOL requires native feature redesign and retraining, not additional deterministic gates. The thin sample (n=7) cannot support a production veto. Additionally, verify no unit contamination (decimal vs percent-point) exists in avg_pnl_pct given recent evolution data normalization fixes.

bridge_results.title: SOL High-Chase Veto Proposal: Statistically Insufficient Sample
bridge_results.why_flagged: Sample size (n=7) is below credible statistical threshold for rule generalization; 100% win rate suggests high overfitting risk; missing reshuffle tail and walk-forward evidence per the MC-tail contract; SOL specifically flagged in recent strategy memory as requiring feature redesign rather than isolated parameter patches.
bridge_results.hypothesis: Reduce late-trend chase losses by vetoing stretched entries near local highs.
bridge_results.risk_note: Deployment risks severe overfitting and false veto signals that could exclude valid entries. Premature rule creation may create selection bias in trend-following logic without robust tail validation.

bridge_results.title: SOL High-Chase Veto: Statistically Underpowered Sample
bridge_results.why_flagged: High-severity 'high_variance' discovery on SOLUSDT high-chase tiered entries with TP50/BE management; confidence 0.77 in DB but sample count (n=7) falls far below Monte Carlo or walk-forward validation standards.
bridge_results.hypothesis: Stretched SOL entries near local highs under trend-follow logic produce volatile, negatively skewed outcomes; selective veto may improve risk-adjusted returns.
bridge_results.risk_note: Recent research indicates SOL requires feature redesign rather than parameter/veto tuning. Acting on n=7 risks false-positive veto that overfits to recent volatility and eliminates valid trend entries during SOL's high-beta regimes.

bridge_results.title: SOL High-Chase Veto Proposal with Statistically Insufficient Sample
bridge_results.why_flagged: High-variance discovery with negative average return in SOLUSDT long entries tagged as high-chase trend-follow behavior.
bridge_results.hypothesis: SOLUSDT long entries in stretched high-chase contexts exhibit negative expectancy due to late-trend exhaustion, and selective vetoing could improve portfolio Sharpe by avoiding these specific entries.
bridge_results.risk_note: Critical false-veto risk: suppressing SOL longs based on 7 samples may discard valid trend entries during high-momentum regimes. Recent Citrus research explicitly flags SOL as requiring feature redesign rather than parameter/veto patching; adding untested deterministic gates compounds architectural uncertainty without addressing root data quality issues.

bridge_results.title: ETH High-Chase Late-Trend Veto (Discovery-22)
bridge_results.why_flagged: Sample size (n=5) is insufficient for production veto rules under current validation standards. Perfect win rate with minimal samples suggests high overfitting risk. Evidence lacks required walk-forward fold stability (target: 20+ folds) and Monte Carlo reshuffle tail metrics (target: worst-5% DD bounds) that define the current MC-tail contract.
bridge_results.hypothesis: Reduce late-trend chase losses by vetoing stretched entries near local highs.
bridge_results.risk_note: Veto rules are absolute blocks in production logic. With n=5, the false negative rate and regime dependency are unknown. No OOS delta vs baseline, no fold breadth, and no reshuffle tail data violate the robustness threshold established in btc_range_sync_core validation profile.

bridge_results.title: ETH Late-Trend Chase Veto (High Variance, n=5)
bridge_results.why_flagged: High severity due to chase-risk hypothesis, but sample size is below statistical threshold for production veto logic. High variance suggests unstable outcomes that could represent noise rather than systematic edge.
bridge_results.hypothesis: Reduce late-trend chase losses by vetoing stretched entries near local highs.
bridge_results.risk_note: n=5 is insufficient for Monte Carlo tail estimation or fold stability assessment; premature production veto risks false negatives on valid entries. Variance magnitude suggests potential outlier dominance in small sample.

bridge_results.title: BTC Long Tiered Entry: High Variance Alert (n=5)
bridge_results.why_flagged: High variance discovery triggered by volatility of returns exceeding expected bounds for the pattern key, with severity marked high despite minimal sample depth.
bridge_results.proposed_change_type: threshold_adjustment (tighten trigger band to reduce entry variance)
bridge_results.hypothesis: Observed variance stems from loose trigger bands allowing inconsistent entries, but current evidence is too thin to justify deterministic gate modification.
bridge_results.risk_note: Sample size of 5 provides insufficient statistical power to distinguish genuine structural variance from noise. Approving threshold adjustments risks overfitting to random fluctuation and eroding edge detection.

bridge_results.title: BTC Late-Trend Long Veto Proposal - Insufficient Sample Size (n=5)
bridge_results.why_flagged: Flagged by high_variance discovery algorithm due to volatile negative returns in a known-problematic regime (late-trend chasing), though sample size falls below minimum threshold for statistical significance.
bridge_results.hypothesis: Late-trend tiered long entries in BTCUSDT near local highs exhibit negative expectancy due to exhaustion; a deterministic veto would improve portfolio risk-adjusted returns by filtering these high-variance chase signals.
bridge_results.risk_note: Critical sample size limitation creates Type I error risk. High variance (9.17) with small samples suggests randomness rather than structural edge. Cannot validate via walk-forward or reshuffle tests at this depth.

bridge_results.title: BTC Late-Trend Chase Veto Proposal (Thin Sample)
bridge_results.why_flagged: High-severity discovery matching the anti-chase failure mode: entries initiated late in trend structure with price extended and proximity to local highs, consistent with historical Grapes transition-zone losses.
bridge_results.hypothesis: Long entries in late-trend context with price z-score >1.0 and proximity to local highs (<2%) exhibit negative expectancy due to trend exhaustion, warranting selective veto once sample robustness is established.
bridge_results.risk_note: Critical sample size limitation (n=5) makes pattern indistinguishable from noise or single-regime artifact. Production veto would risk false positive and overfitting. Monte Carlo and walk-forward evidence unavailable at this sample depth. Requires minimum 20-30 additional observations or explicit regime perturbation before shadow testing.

bridge_results.title: BTC Late-Trend Veto Proposal: Critical Sample Size Insufficiency
bridge_results.why_flagged: Sample count (n=6) is insufficient for Monte Carlo validation or walk-forward stability assessment. Perfect win rate (1.0) on tiny samples is a classic overfitting indicator. Current Grapes validation standards require 200+ trades and 20+ folds for production rules.
bridge_results.hypothesis: Reduce late-trend chase losses by vetoing stretched entries near local highs.
bridge_results.risk_note: Deploying a veto with n=6 risks severe false-positive tail risk. The pattern could veto legitimate trend-following entries during strong momentum phases, degrading portfolio Sharpe. Reshuffle tail metrics are impossible to compute at this sample size.

bridge_results.title: BTC Late-Trend Tiered Entry: High Variance on Insufficient Sample
bridge_results.why_flagged: Sample count (n=6) is below the threshold required for robust statistical inference. High PnL variance suggests the pattern may capture noise or regime-specific outliers rather than a consistent structural edge. The reported confidence (0.76) overstates reliability given the sample size.
bridge_results.proposed_change_type: Defer veto implementation; collect additional samples through shadow tracking or extended observation window to confirm pattern stability.
bridge_results.hypothesis: Late-trend tiered entries in BTC near local highs exhibit deteriorating risk/reward ratios due to exhaustion effects; a selective veto could improve portfolio Sharpe if the pattern holds across larger sample and distinct market regimes.
bridge_results.risk_note: Implementing a production veto on 6 samples risks false-positive filtering and overfitting to noise. High variance indicates asymmetric tail risk that could just as easily represent profitable volatility as toxic chase behavior.

bridge_results.title: BTC High-Chase Veto Proposal (n=6)
bridge_results.why_flagged: Positive edge detection in 'btcusdt:high_chase.tp50_then_be.trend_follow.tiered_entry' pattern with perfect win rate but critically small sample depth.
bridge_results.hypothesis: Reduce late-trend chase losses by vetoing stretched entries near local highs.
bridge_results.risk_note: Extreme overfitting risk. A production veto derived from 6 samples may prematurely block valid trend entries or create false confidence without Monte Carlo or walk-forward validation. Reshuffle tail behavior is undefined.

bridge_results.title: BTC High-Chase Veto Proposal (Insufficient Sample)
bridge_results.why_flagged: High-severity 'high_variance' discovery with n=6 sample size proposing a deterministic veto gate; risk of spurious pattern detection and overfitting to noise.
bridge_results.hypothesis: Late-trend entries near local highs in BTC show characteristically poor risk-adjusted returns and should be vetoed to reduce chase losses.
bridge_results.risk_note: Current evidence (n=6, high variance) lacks fold stability and reshuffle-tail validation required by the current MC-tail contract. The 4.97% avg PnL may be driven by 1-2 outliers. Deploying as a deterministic veto would violate production evidence standards.

bridge_results.title: ETH Crowded Shorts Trend-Follow: Minimal Sample Shadow Candidate
bridge_results.why_flagged: Positive edge detection triggered by perfect win rate on pattern 'ethusdt:crowded_shorts.trend_follow.tiered_entry.short' within validation profile 'btc_range_sync_core'.
bridge_results.proposed_change_type: Shadow rule activation for ETH short crowded-shorts tiered entry logic to accumulate evidence without production risk.
bridge_results.hypothesis: ETH short entries during crowded funding conditions may capture asymmetric edge via short-squeeze exhaustion or funding arbitrage dynamics, but current evidence is anecdotal and requires broader validation.
bridge_results.risk_note: Sample size of 6 provides no statistical power; 100% win rate likely reflects noise, recent regime bias, or volatility clustering rather than durable alpha. High overfitting risk if promoted prematurely.

bridge_results.title: ETHUSDT Crowded Shorts High Variance (n=6)
bridge_results.why_flagged: High severity variance alert on tiered entry logic with minimal sample support (n=6).
bridge_results.hypothesis: Crowded short regime produces high-variance entry clusters that may stabilize with tighter trigger thresholds, but this requires validation beyond the current 6-sample footprint.
bridge_results.risk_note: Sample size of 6 provides statistically unreliable variance estimates. Acting on high-variance clusters with sparse data risks overfitting to noise and eliminating valid entries during regime transitions. ETHUSDT is a high-liquidity core pair where threshold errors propagate to portfolio level.

bridge_results.title: ETHUSDT High-Chase Trend Entry: High Variance on Critical Sample Limit (n=6)
bridge_results.why_flagged: Discovery heuristic 'high_variance' triggered by elevated pnl_variance relative to mean return, combined with 'high_chase' pattern classification in trend-following context.
bridge_results.proposed_change_type: new_veto (deterministic gate on ETHUSDT long entries near local highs in trend-follow regime)
bridge_results.hypothesis: Reduce late-trend chase losses by vetoing stretched entries near local highs.
bridge_results.risk_note: Severe sample size insufficiency (n=6) creates unacceptable Type I error risk. The high variance metric may reflect noise or outlier luck rather than structural market behavior. Implementing a veto on this evidence would violate the MC-tail and walk-forward validation standards required for production promotion (see btc_range_sync_core and eth_high_chase precedent).

bridge_results.title: SOL Late-Trend Veto Proposal (n=7)
bridge_results.why_flagged: Critically small sample size (n=7) with perfect win rate indicates high overfitting risk; SOL is explicitly flagged in current research as requiring native feature redesign rather than additional veto logic or parameter tuning.
bridge_results.hypothesis: Reduce late-trend chase losses by vetoing stretched entries near local highs.
bridge_results.risk_note: Deploying veto logic on 7 samples risks false positives and overfitting to noise; contradicts current stance that SOL needs feature redesign, not band-aid veto rules; premature implementation could filter valid entries without structural justification.

bridge_results.title: SOL Late-Trend Long Veto Proposal (n=9)
bridge_results.why_flagged: Automated high-variance discovery triggered on mean PnL -1.18% with variance 21.01, but sample count (9) falls below robust validation thresholds.
bridge_results.proposed_change_type: Shadow logging to collect minimum 50+ samples before validation; defer veto implementation pending MC tail and fold stability evidence.
bridge_results.hypothesis: SOL late-trend long entries near local highs exhibit negative expectancy suitable for veto once validated with sufficient statistical depth and cross-fold stability.
bridge_results.risk_note: Extreme overfitting risk with n=9. Recent Citrus memory indicates SOL requires feature redesign rather than parameter-level gates; premature vetoes risk false security on incomplete data.

bridge_results.title: SOL Late-Trend Chase Shadow Veto
bridge_results.why_flagged: High cluster confidence (0.81) combined with classic exhaustion signature: proximity to highs, elevated range position, and negative price momentum in a stretched long context. Matches known anti-chase failure mode.
bridge_results.proposed_change_type: Shadow veto rule for SOL longs: suppress entries when distance_to_high_pct < 5%, range_position > 0.75, and dz_price < 0 simultaneously during late_trend regime.
bridge_results.hypothesis: SOL long entries within 5% of local highs with negative momentum and high range position represent trend-exhaustion chase behavior with degraded expectancy.
bridge_results.risk_note: Critical sample size limitation (n=9) creates overfitting risk to recent SOL volatility. Pattern may generate false positives during legitimate high-momentum breakouts. Note: SOL flagged for feature redesign in Citrus research; this veto treats symptom (chase losses) while structural work proceeds.

bridge_results.title: ETH Late-Trend High-Chase Veto (Sample-Starved)
bridge_results.why_flagged: Positive edge detection (confidence 0.81) against btc_range_sync_core profile matches active anti-chase research priorities, but sample scarcity (n=11) prevents reliable inference. Missing walk-forward fold stability, Monte Carlo tail behavior, and OOS delta versus baseline.
bridge_results.hypothesis: Vetoing ETHUSDT entries near local highs during late-trend phases captures a repeatable anti-chase edge distinct from existing gates.
bridge_results.risk_note: Severe overfitting risk due to n=11. Pattern may represent transient regime noise or survival bias rather than structural edge. Premature veto deployment risks false negatives (unnecessarily filtered entries) if the signal decays. Requires minimum 50+ samples and Monte Carlo reshuffle tail validation before promotion consideration.
